* [1929 Palestine riots](/wiki/1929_Palestine_riots "1929 Palestine riots") * [1936–1939 Arab revolt in Palestine](/wiki/1936%E2%80%931939_Arab_revolt_in_Palestine "1936–1939 Arab revolt in Palestine") * [1948 Palestine war](/wiki/1948_Palestine_war "1948 Palestine war")

* Director of the licensing department at the [Ministry of Transportation](/wiki/Ministry_of_Transport_and_Road_Safety "Ministry of Transport and Road Safety") * Auditor of the [Tel Aviv Municipality](/wiki/Tel_Aviv-Yafo_Municipality "Tel Aviv-Yafo Municipality")

([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): צבי איָילון; 15 June 1911 - 4 March 1993)[[1]](#cite_note-1) was a [Haganah](/wiki/Haganah "Haganah") leader and a major general ([Aluf](/wiki/Aluf "Aluf")) in the [Israel Defense Forces](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces"), he served as the first [Deputy Chief of General Staff](/wiki/Deputy_Chief_of_General_Staff_(IDF) "Deputy Chief of General Staff (IDF)") as well as the commander of the [Central Command (Israel)](/wiki/Central_Command_(Israel) "Central Command (Israel)"). After his military service he served as the deputy director-general of the [Ministry of Defense (Israel)](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defense_(Israel) "Ministry of Defense (Israel)") and [Ambassador of Israel in Romania](/wiki/Israel%E2%80%93Romania_relations "Israel–Romania relations").

["צבי גרישה איילון 15 June 1911 - 4 March 1993 in BillionGraves GPS Headstones | BillionGraves"](https://billiongraves.com/grave/%D7%A6%D7%91%D7%99-%D7%92%D7%A8%D7%99%D7%A9%D7%94-%D7%90%D7%99%D7%99%D7%9C%D7%95%D7%9F/17195583). *billiongraves.com*. Retrieved 2023-01-18.

["⁨Untitled⁩ — ⁨⁨מעריב⁩ 17 יולי 1949⁩ — הספרייה הלאומית של ישראל │ עיתונים"](https://www.nli.org.il/he/newspapers/mar/1949/07/17/01/article/14). *www.nli.org.il* (in Hebrew). Retrieved 2023-01-18.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Zvi Ayalon](/w/index.php?title=Zvi_Ayalon&redirect=no "Zvi Ayalon"))

First Israeli Defense Forces Deputy Chief of Staff

| Aluf   Tzvi Ayalon | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Native name | צבי איילון |
| Born | Tzvi Lashchiner  15 June 1911  [Russian Empire](/wiki/Russian_Empire "Russian Empire") |
| Died | 4 March 1993 (aged 81)  [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel") |
| Allegiance | [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel") [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel") |
| Branch | [Haganah](/wiki/Haganah "Haganah")  [Israel Defense Forces](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces") |
| Service years | 1927-1965 (38 years) |
| Rank | [Aluf](/wiki/Aluf "Aluf") (Major General) |
| Commands | * Various Haganah training camps * Haganah commander of the Northern Galilee * [General Staff](/wiki/General_Staff "General Staff") * [Central Command](/wiki/Central_Command_(Israel) "Central Command (Israel)") * [Deputy Chief of Staff](/wiki/Deputy_Chief_of_General_Staff_(IDF) "Deputy Chief of General Staff (IDF)") * [Technological and Logistics Directorate](/wiki/Technological_and_Logistics_Directorate "Technological and Logistics Directorate") |
| Conflicts | * [1929 Palestine riots](/wiki/1929_Palestine_riots "1929 Palestine riots") * [1936–1939 Arab revolt in Palestine](/wiki/1936%E2%80%931939_Arab_revolt_in_Palestine "1936–1939 Arab revolt in Palestine") * [1948 Palestine war](/wiki/1948_Palestine_war "1948 Palestine war") |
| Other work | * Director of the licensing department at the [Ministry of Transportation](/wiki/Ministry_of_Transport_and_Road_Safety "Ministry of Transport and Road Safety") * Auditor of the [Tel Aviv Municipality](/wiki/Tel_Aviv-Yafo_Municipality "Tel Aviv-Yafo Municipality") |

**Tzvi Ayalon** ([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): צבי איָילון; 15 June 1911 - 4 March 1993)[[1]](#cite_note-1) was a [Haganah](/wiki/Haganah "Haganah") leader and a major general ([Aluf](/wiki/Aluf "Aluf")) in the [Israel Defense Forces](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces"), he served as the first [Deputy Chief of General Staff](/wiki/Deputy_Chief_of_General_Staff_(IDF) "Deputy Chief of General Staff (IDF)") as well as the commander of the [Central Command (Israel)](/wiki/Central_Command_(Israel) "Central Command (Israel)"). After his military service he served as the deputy director-general of the [Ministry of Defense (Israel)](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defense_(Israel) "Ministry of Defense (Israel)") and [Ambassador of Israel in Romania](/wiki/Israel%E2%80%93Romania_relations "Israel–Romania relations").

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tzvi_Ayalon&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

### Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tzvi_Ayalon&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early life")]

Ayalon was born in the [Russian Empire](/wiki/Russian_Empire "Russian Empire") in the summer of 1911, son of Esther and Meir Lashchiner. In 1923, as a young teenager, he immigrated to the [British mandate of Palestine](/wiki/Mandatory_Palestine "Mandatory Palestine"). Studied at the [Reali School in Haifa](/wiki/Hebrew_Reali_School "Hebrew Reali School") and at the [Mikveh Israel Agricultural School](/wiki/Mikveh_Israel "Mikveh Israel").

### Haganah service

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tzvi_Ayalon&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Haganah service")]

At the age of 16, he joined the Jewish paramilitary organization [Haganah](/wiki/Haganah "Haganah"). He served as a platoon commander during the [1929 Palestine riots](/wiki/1929_Palestine_riots "1929 Palestine riots"); in which he was injured. He served from 1936 in the permanent apparatus of the [Haganah](/wiki/Haganah "Haganah") in the [Haifa area](/wiki/Haifa "Haifa"), and held command and training positions in the area, and later in the [northern Galilee](/wiki/Galilee "Galilee") area. In 1943 he instructed the officers' course of the [Haganah](/wiki/Haganah "Haganah") organization in [Joara](/wiki/Jo%27ara "Jo'ara"). In 1944 he was appointed commander of the Northern Galilee. Later he was a senior staff officer in the General Staff. He was a partner in formulating the idea of the Field Force ([Hish](/wiki/Hish_(Haganah_corps) "Hish (Haganah corps)")) and the combat theory of 'offensive defense'. On the eve of the outbreak of the War of Independence, he served as a planning officer at the General Staff.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/Ayalon_avidar.jpeg/250px-Ayalon_avidar.jpeg)](/wiki/File:Ayalon_avidar.jpeg)

Tzvi Ayalon (on the right) with [Yosef Avidar](/wiki/Yosef_Avidar "Yosef Avidar"), 1949

### IDF service

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tzvi_Ayalon&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: IDF service")]

In January 1948, he was appointed [Deputy Chief of Staff](/wiki/Deputy_Chief_of_General_Staff_(IDF) "Deputy Chief of General Staff (IDF)"), and with the establishment of the [IDF](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces") in May of that year, he continued in his position, with the rank of Major General ([Aluf](/wiki/Aluf "Aluf")). At the same time as his position as [Deputy Chief of Staff](/wiki/Deputy_Chief_of_General_Staff_(IDF) "Deputy Chief of General Staff (IDF)"), he was the commander of the Third Front (today the [Central Command](/wiki/Central_Command_(Israel) "Central Command (Israel)")), which commanded the forces that [fought in and around Jerusalem](/wiki/Battle_for_Jerusalem "Battle for Jerusalem").

Due to the illness of the [Chief of Staff](/wiki/Chief_of_the_General_Staff_(Israel) "Chief of the General Staff (Israel)") [Yaakov Dori](/wiki/Yaakov_Dori "Yaakov Dori"), he was the acting [Chief of Staff](/wiki/Chief_of_the_General_Staff_(Israel) "Chief of the General Staff (Israel)") for a while. While acting as Chief of Staff, he signed arrest warrants for the [Altalena](/wiki/Altalena "Altalena") defendants[[2]](#cite_note-2) as well as [Meir Tobiansky](/wiki/Meir_Tobianski "Meir Tobianski")'s arrest warrant.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

After the war he was responsible for the creation of the [IDF parade](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces_parade "Israel Defense Forces parade") in July 1949, which took place after the failure of the parade that did not march on the [Independence Day](/wiki/Independence_Day_(Israel) "Independence Day (Israel)").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 1952 he was appointed head of logistics department (today [Technological and Logistics Directorate](/wiki/Technological_and_Logistics_Directorate "Technological and Logistics Directorate")) and in 1954 he was appointed as commander of the [Central Command (Israel)](/wiki/Central_Command_(Israel) "Central Command (Israel)"), a position which he served in until February 1956.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8d/D358-072_Sharet%2C_Ben-Gurion%2C_Yadin_and_others.jpg/250px-D358-072_Sharet%2C_Ben-Gurion%2C_Yadin_and_others.jpg)](/wiki/File:D358-072_Sharet,_Ben-Gurion,_Yadin_and_others.jpg)

Prime Minister David Ben-Gurion, Foreign Minister [Moshe Sharett](/wiki/Moshe_Sharett "Moshe Sharett"), Chief of Staff [Yigal Yadin](/wiki/Yigael_Yadin "Yigael Yadin") and senior Israeli army officers attended a ceremony for Aluf Maklef. Tzvi Ayalon (on the left behind Ben-Gurion)

In August 1957 he was appointed head of the Construction and Assets Division at the [Ministry of Defense](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defense_(Israel) "Ministry of Defense (Israel)"),[[5]](#cite_note-5) in July 1958 he was appointed by then [Prime Minister and Minister of Defense David Ben-Gurion](/wiki/David_Ben-Gurion "David Ben-Gurion") as Deputy Director of the economic affairs of the [Ministry of Defense](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defense_(Israel) "Ministry of Defense (Israel)"),[[6]](#cite_note-6) and in 1964 he served as the [Ambassador](/wiki/Israel%E2%80%93Romania_relations "Israel–Romania relations") of [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel") in [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### Other work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tzvi_Ayalon&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Other work")]

He officially left his military service in 1965 and became the director of the licensing department at the [Ministry of Transportation](/wiki/Ministry_of_Transport_and_Road_Safety "Ministry of Transport and Road Safety") and as auditor of the [Tel Aviv Municipality](/wiki/Tel_Aviv-Yafo_Municipality "Tel Aviv-Yafo Municipality").[[8]](#cite_note-8) in 1968 he co-founded the [Council for a Beautiful Israel](/wiki/Council_for_a_Beautiful_Israel "Council for a Beautiful Israel").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Awards and decorations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tzvi_Ayalon&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Awards and decorations")]

Tzvi Ayalon was awarded one [campaign ribbons](/wiki/Campaign_medal "Campaign medal") for his service.

|  |
| --- |
|  |

|  |
| --- |
| [Israeli Independence war](/wiki/1948_Palestine_war "1948 Palestine war") |

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tzvi_Ayalon&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In 1931 he married Rachel Katznelson (who died in the spring of 1970), and was the father of two.

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tzvi_Ayalon&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: References")]
